perturbation.original_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief .
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be first attempted to be resolved through mediation in Seoul, Korea. If mediation fails, the dispute shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief .
perturbation.explanation: Here, a contradiction is introduced by mandating mediation before arbitration. The original text specifies that disputes 'shall be finally resolved by arbitration'. The added clause now states disputes should 'first' be attempted to be resolved through mediation, creating uncertainty about which dispute resolution process takes precedence and if both must occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea (excluding conflict of laws provisions which may direct the application of another jurisdiction’s laws). All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be first attempted to be resolved through mediation in Seoul, Korea. If mediation fails, the dispute shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief . The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties. Except to the extent entry of judgment and any subsequent enforcement may require disclosure, all matters relating to the arbitration, including the award, shall be held in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a requirement for mediation before arbitration, while the original text stated disputes 'shall be finally resolved by arbitration'. The contradicted text also includes the new mediation clause. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the dispute resolution process, as it's now uncertain whether mediation is mandatory before arbitration, making it an inconsistency as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be amended or modified by email communication between authorized representatives of each party, or in writing signed by each of the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: A contradiction is created by allowing modifications via email. The original text states the agreement 'may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties'. The changed text adds the option of amendment via 'email communication between authorized representatives', which contradicts the requirement for a signed written agreement, leading to uncertainty over valid amendment methods.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be amended or modified by email communication between authorized representatives of each party, or in writing signed by each of the parties to this Agreement. The English language text of this Agreement shall prevail over any translations thereof. If a contract is entered into by the parties in respect of the Purpose, this Agreement shall be deemed incorporated into such contract in addition to any confidential ity obligations set forth in such contract, if any , and the term of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the contract .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments must be in writing and signed by all parties. The changed text introduces email communication as an alternative method for amendments. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, which now contradicts the original requirement for signed written agreements. This creates a clear inconsistency, leading to uncertainty about valid amendment methods and therefore a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, “affiliate ” means an entity that , as of Effective Date , directly or indirectly, controls or is under common control with a party to this Agreement, but only for so long as such control exists , and where “control” shall mean ownership of more than 50% of the stock or other equity interests entitled to vote for the election of directors or an equivalent governing body.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, “affiliate ” means an entity that , as of Effective Date , directly or indirectly, controls or is under common control with a party to this Agreement, but only for so long as such control exists , and where “control” shall mean ownership of more than 25% of the stock or other equity interests entitled to vote for the election of directors or an equivalent governing body.
perturbation.explanation: A contradiction is created by redefining the threshold for 'control' in determining affiliate status. The original text sets the threshold at 'more than 50%', whereas the changed text lowers it to 'more than 25%'. This creates an in-text contradiction because other sections may rely on the original 50% threshold, leading to confusion about which definition of 'affiliate' and 'control' applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information” includes a ll business, financial, contractual, marketing and/or technical information, in whatever form embodied, which has been or may be disclosed , or to which access is provided, by a party (“Discloser”) to the other party to this Agreement (“Recipient”) , which (a) if in wri ting, is marked “confidential”, “proprietary” or other similar marking at the time of disclosure , or (b) if provided orally or visually, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and confirmed in writing to Recipient within 15 days of such disclosure . Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to Recipient’s affiliates on the condition that Recipient shall restrict access to Discloser’s Confidential Information to those of Recipient’s affiliates’ officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need -to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Discloser than those contained in this Agreement and Recipient shall be liable for any failure of its affiliates to abide by the provisions of this Agreement as if such failure was the act or omiss ion of such party. For the purposes of this Agreement, “affiliate ” means an entity that , as of Effective Date , directly or indirectly, controls or is under common control with a party to this Agreement, but only for so long as such control exists , and where “control” shall mean ownership of more than 25% of the stock or other equity interests entitled to vote for the election of directors or an equivalent governing body.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'control' from 'more than 50%' to 'more than 25%' in the context of determining affiliate status. The contradicted text also defines 'affiliate' using the same definition, but now the two definitions of control are different, causing a direct in-text contradiction according to the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction perturbation type. Both sections rely on the definition of 'control' to determine which entities are considered affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules and, in particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules, except for the re-export of Confidential Information within South Korea where no export licenses are required. In particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the export compliance clause. By stating 'except for the re-export of Confidential Information within South Korea where no export licenses are required', it contradicts the general requirement in the same sentence of not re-exporting Confidential Information without obtaining required licenses. This creates an in-text contradiction. What happens, in case if the re-export isn't being made within South Korea.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Export . Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules, except for the re-export of Confidential Information within South Korea where no export licenses are required. In particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception ('except for the re-export of Confidential Information within South Korea where no export licenses are required') to the general rule of compliance with export laws and the requirement to obtain government licenses for re-exporting Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in the same sentence that says, 'In particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers', creating a clear in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about whether licenses are needed in all situations, particularly outside South Korea.
